actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour_0 maps to Fire (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) on GameObject 'Fire-Wrapper - R - 2 - 2'. BoxCollider enabled, no Rigidbody. No UnityEvent m_Calls exposed. Serialized: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5. This trigger is a timed wait to allow automatic Update() behavior when the neighbor condition is satisfied (onNeighbors >= 2) so shouldTurnOn becomes true and the fire turns on after timeToOn elapses. Expected behavior upon auto ignition: UpdateFireState(true) => ParticleSystem.Play; NotifyAllNeighbors(); FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1). Requires: at least two neighbor Fire instances within 4 units and already on; or that FindNeighbors/neighbor wiring be completed externally.

Turn-off path: while isOn==true, repeated WaterHit(Vector3) calls must occur so currentHitInterval < maxHitInterval (2s) until currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s), then Update() turns fire off and calls UpdateFireState(true) => ParticleSystem.Stop; NotifyAllNeighbors(); FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: Fire has no public parameterless methods and no UnityEvent m_Calls; WaterHit(Vector3) requires a parameter and cannot be invoked via a generic Trigger per constraints. A water system or test hook must invoke Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) repeatedly during this 5.5s window.
